response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with strong general software craftsmanship, but is a poor match for this embedded networking security role. The position hard-requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, and Linux kernel source-level analysis — none of which are evidenced. Their C/C++ exposure is brief (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago), and there is no experience with L2/L3 switching, MACsec, 802.1X, or embedded firmware for networking hardware.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with systems-level exposure (DLang low-latency services, some C/C++)
Strong testing culture and software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, open-source tooling)
Self-described embedded experience and Linux familiarity
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement
No demonstrated C-for-UNIX depth or Linux kernel modification experience
No networking security background (MACsec, 802.1X, switch configuration, SNMP/NETCONF)

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded networking/security domain experience
General seniority and systems programming exposure
